intent: systemd (init_t, pid 1) denied read+open on a unit-referenced file labeled default_t (e.g. an EnvironmentFile= path that did not get a service-specific label). Maps to one clean narrow allow; audit2allow -R substitutes the broad files_read_default_files() interface.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic).

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R substitutes files_read_default_files() (read_file_perms padding on ALL default_t files + default_t dir list) for init_t. Our expected.te emits exactly `allow init_t default_t:file { read open };` - the 2 denied perms only, no macro, no padding, no dir grant.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl mirroring -R would emit files_read_default_files(init_t), granting pid-1 read of every default_t file. The narrow fix is the 2 denied perms on the one class. The real-world remedy is usually a correct label on the referenced path (e.g. a drop-in with the right fcontext) rather than a permanent init_t allow.
